appraisal_events.evidence: « une vie crapuleuse racontée par la déchéance morale »; « Il débordait maintenant dans ses propos. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator presents Charlus as physically and morally degraded, with vice made visible in his appearance and spilling into his speech, locally lowering his standing.

appraisal_events.evidence: « Il reconnaissait immédiatement ce à quoi personne n’eût jamais fait attention »; « je regrette encore, que baron de Charlus n’ait jamais rien écrit… le feu eût jailli »; detailed, apt advice on Albertine’s dress and materials.
appraisal_events.explanation: Despite moral censure, the narrator elevates Charlus as a keen observer and connoisseur with precise nomenclature and potential literary brilliance, illustrated by his exacting fashion judgments about Albertine.

status_effects.explanation: He is locally diminished by the narrator’s depiction of visible moral and physical decline.
He gains intellectual/aesthetic authority through the narrator’s praise of his observational acuity and discriminating taste.
